summary: DllNotFoundException for libSkiaSharp in a WASI Wasm Console app because SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly only supports browser-based WASM (Blazor/Uno) via Emscripten static linking, not the WASI runtime.

stepsToReproduce: Create a .NET 9 WASI Wasm Console app with wasi-wasm RID
Add SkiaSharp and SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly 3.0.0-preview.4.1 package references

attachments.description: Minimal WASI Wasm repro project with built .wasm file

reproEvidence.environmentDetails: .NET 9 preview 6, wasi-wasm RID, Visual Studio (Windows), SkiaSharp 3.0.0-preview.4.1

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: WASI is still not a supported target. The NativeAssets.WebAssembly MSBuild targets have not changed to include WASI support.

analysis.summary: The NativeAssets.WebAssembly package only activates static linking for Blazor (UsingMicrosoftNETSdkBlazorWebAssembly) and Uno (IsUnoHead + UnoRuntimeIdentifier==WebAssembly). A WASI Wasm Console app sets neither property, so the .a files are never passed to the linker. Additionally, the shipped .a files are Emscripten-compiled targeting browser WASM APIs, which are incompatible with the WASI runtime even if linking were configured.
analysis.rationale: While filed as a bug, WASI WASM is a fundamentally different compilation target from browser-based WASM. The NativeAssets.WebAssembly package was designed for Emscripten/browser WASM (Blazor, Uno). Supporting WASI would require recompiling libSkiaSharp with a WASI-compatible toolchain and adding new MSBuild conditions. This is a new platform support request, not a defect in existing functionality.

keySignals.text: The NativeAssets has build targets for Blazor, but seems to be missing them for Wasi Wasm apps
keySignals.interpretation: Reporter correctly identified the gap — MSBuild targets only activate for Blazor/Uno, not WASI.
Expected failure — the native library was never linked into the WASI binary because MSBuild conditions excluded it.

keySignals.text: The 3.x series should be including the native assets. Have you tried that yet?
keySignals.interpretation: Maintainer asked about 3.x, but reporter's csproj already shows 3.0.0-preview.4.1. The 3.x series includes WASM native assets for Blazor/Uno but not WASI.

codeInvestigation.finding: NativeFileReference items are only added when UsingMicrosoftNETSdkBlazorWebAssembly or UsingMicrosoftNETSdkWebAssembly is true. A WASI Wasm Console app sets neither, so the .a files are never linked.
The Uno Platform path also does not apply — it requires IsUnoHead==True and UnoRuntimeIdentifier==WebAssembly.
Props file defines SkiaSharpStaticLibraryPath pointing to the Emscripten-compiled .a files. These are browser WASM binaries, not WASI-compatible.

nextQuestions: Is WASI a target SkiaSharp intends to support?
Would Skia need to be compiled with wasi-sdk instead of Emscripten for WASI compatibility?
Are there Skia upstream efforts for WASI support?

resolution.hypothesis: WASI is a distinct compilation target from browser WASM. The Emscripten-compiled static libraries in NativeAssets.WebAssembly are not compatible with WASI. Supporting WASI would require compiling libSkiaSharp with a WASI-compatible toolchain (wasi-sdk/WASI libc) and adding MSBuild integration for the wasi-wasm RID.

proposals.title: Add WASI-specific NativeAssets package or RID support
proposals.description: Compile libSkiaSharp with wasi-sdk targeting WASI, and either extend NativeAssets.WebAssembly to detect wasi-wasm RID or create a new NativeAssets.Wasi package. This requires Skia to build under wasi-sdk which may have significant upstream dependencies.

proposals.title: Document WASI as unsupported
proposals.description: Update documentation to clarify that NativeAssets.WebAssembly targets browser-based WASM only (Blazor, Uno) and that WASI is not currently supported.

resolution.recommendedReason: WASI support is a significant engineering effort requiring upstream Skia compatibility. Clarifying documentation helps set correct expectations while WASI matures in the .NET ecosystem.

actionability.reason: Reporter is already using 3.x preview. The maintainer's question about 3.x may be moot. Clarify that WASI is not supported and ask if there's a specific use case driving WASI over Blazor WASM.

actions.description: Reclassify as feature request for WASI platform support
Explain WASI vs browser WASM distinction and current support scope
actions.comment:
Thanks for the report and the repro project.

The `SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly` package currently supports browser-based WASM only — specifically Blazor and Uno Platform apps. The native `.a` libraries are compiled with Emscripten, which targets browser APIs. A WASI Console app (`wasi-wasm` RID) uses a fundamentally different runtime (WASI system calls instead of browser/Emscripten APIs), so the Emscripten-compiled libraries aren't compatible.

For reference, the MSBuild targets in the NativeAssets.WebAssembly package only activate when `UsingMicrosoftNETSdkBlazorWebAssembly` or `UsingMicrosoftNETSdkWebAssembly` is set, which a WASI Console app doesn't set.

Supporting WASI would require recompiling libSkiaSharp with a WASI-compatible toolchain (e.g., wasi-sdk). Could you share more about your use case? That would help us evaluate prioritizing WASI support.
